cards.title: Buyer Rescue Workflow
cards.thesis: Convert a support failure into a reviewed workflow improvement that protects the buyer and creates reusable trust.
cards.plain: A buyer cannot access a deliverable. Instead of treating the reply as a one-off support message, GoalOS turns the problem into a proof mission: improve the support workflow, validate it, publish a public-safe Proof Card, and reuse only the approved artifact in future support work.
cards.why: This is the simplest proof that AI work can become an institutional asset. Customer support is understandable to everyone, and a small improvement can create visible trust, lower support cost, and produce the first reusable proof artifact.
cards.mission: Improve the Buyer Rescue Workflow from a helpful but incomplete response into a repeatable, reviewed, claim-safe support procedure.

cards.title: Sovereign Procurement Trust Room
cards.thesis: Turn enterprise due diligence into a proof mission that produces a reusable trust-room artifact.
cards.plain: A serious buyer asks whether the system can be trusted. GoalOS converts that buyer concern into a structured proof mission: collect the evidence, define the claim boundary, review the result, and publish a public-safe trust room.
cards.why: Procurement friction blocks adoption. A trust room lowers buyer uncertainty by making evidence visible, organized, and reviewable without exposing private operational data.
cards.mission: Create a public-safe procurement trust room that explains status, evidence, boundaries, risks, contracts, and the next gate.

cards.title: Sovereign AI Procurement Control Tower
cards.thesis: Create a control tower for evaluating, governing, approving, and rolling back AI workflow adoption.
cards.plain: A sovereign or enterprise buyer needs to know whether an AI workflow system can be trusted, governed, audited, rolled back, and improved safely. This Proof Card converts that into a governed control tower with evidence, review, and upgrade rights.
cards.why: AI adoption fails when buyers cannot inspect the operating surface. A control tower gives executives one place to see what was tested, who reviewed it, what failed, what passed, and what can be safely reused.
cards.mission: Build a procurement control tower that shows the proof route from mission definition to evidence, reviewer approval, selection, rollout, and rollback.

cards.title: Persistent Goal-Seeking Intelligence Accumulation Engine
cards.thesis: Frame GoalOS AGIALPHA Ascension as an experimental framework for accumulating capability, evidence, and economic value over time.
cards.plain: This card introduces the persistent layer: the system is not a one-off agent demo. It is an experimental framework that keeps memory of goals, evidence, artifacts, outcomes, reviews, credentials, and what may shape future work.
cards.why: A persistent intelligence system becomes valuable when it remembers what worked, what failed, who proved it, and which artifacts deserve reuse. This is the first step toward compounding institutional intelligence.
cards.mission: Create a persistent proof memory that links goals, runs, artifacts, reviews, proof cards, credentials, reputation, and future routing.

cards.title: Sovereign RSI Value-to-Capability Treasury
cards.thesis: Route verified value into stronger future capability budgets.
cards.plain: Proof Card 005 adds the reinvestment layer: verified work can generate value, and that value can be governed and routed into better missions, audits, tools, reviewers, and future capabilities.
cards.why: A self-improving institution needs a disciplined treasury loop. Without governance, value leaks into noise. With evidence-based allocation, verified value strengthens the next round of capability creation.
cards.mission: Design a Value-to-Capability Treasury that connects proof outcomes, AlphaWorkUnits, reviewer confidence, and AGIALPHA settlement to future mission budgets.

cards.title: Sovereign Enterprise RSI Operating System
cards.thesis: Coordinate large specialist-agent institutions so verified work becomes sovereign enterprise operating capacity.
cards.plain: This card upgrades from individual proof missions to an enterprise operating system. Multiple agent cells coordinate through evidence, review, AGIALPHA settlement, rollback readiness, and Chronicle memory.
cards.why: Enterprises need more than clever agents. They need repeatable operating capacity: bounded roles, evidence, approvals, budgets, audits, rollback, and reusable capability.
cards.mission: Model a large specialist-agent operating system for converting enterprise work into evidence-backed reusable capability.

cards.title: Sovereign Enterprise Autoresearch Reactor
cards.thesis: Turn automated research loops into a governed enterprise reactor with proof, review, settlement, rollback, and Chronicle memory.
cards.plain: This card shows how GoalOS-native Ascension can run many improvement threads, test ideas, validate results, red-team evidence, and promote only proven artifacts into future workflows.
cards.why: Autoresearch becomes commercially powerful when discoveries are not just found, but proven, reviewed, priced, settled, remembered, and safely reused across institutional workflows.
cards.mission: Create an enterprise autoresearch reactor that converts strategic goals into parallel proof missions and accepted artifacts into scoped upgrade rights.

cards.title: Sovereign Corporate Capability Foundry
cards.thesis: Convert proof missions across sovereign corporate domains into a capability foundry that compounds accepted artifacts.
cards.plain: This card takes the autoresearch loop and turns it into a corporate foundry: procurement, finance, compliance, security, operations, energy, compute, and R&D each become lanes for proof missions and reusable capabilities.
cards.why: Large corporations need systematic capability production, not isolated automation experiments. A foundry model creates a portfolio of verified artifacts that can be reused, audited, improved, and funded.
cards.mission: Build a portfolio-level foundry for allocating AGIALPHA-coordinated proof missions across high-value sovereign corporate domains.

cards.title: Cyber-Sovereign Execution Moat
cards.thesis: Turn authorized cyber weaknesses into proof-backed defensive capability that compounds over time.
cards.plain: This card brings the system into a high-value cyber-sovereign domain. Authorized weaknesses become bounded missions, specialist agents propose remediations, harnesses test them, reviewers and red teams validate them, and only proven remediations can shape future operations.
cards.why: Cyber defense is an execution moat because trust depends on evidence, speed, replayability, and safe rollout. A cyber proof system must prevent unverified fixes from becoming operational context.
cards.mission: Create a cyber-sovereign execution moat using authorized defensive remediation, regression harnesses, red-team challenge, rollback paths, and evidence-backed upgrade rights.

cards.title: Cyber-Sovereign Proof Benchmark Market
cards.thesis: Turn cyber-sovereign work into replayable proof benchmarks with independent reproduction and AGIALPHA settlement.
cards.plain: This card converts cyber execution into a benchmark market. A task must have a manifest, baseline, evaluator harness, ProofBundle, reviewer report, red-team challenge, reproduction receipt, and Selection Certificate before it can influence future operations.
cards.why: Benchmark-visible execution is stronger than claims. A replayable benchmark market makes progress legible to buyers, reviewers, engineers, and sponsors.
cards.mission: Create a cyber-sovereign proof benchmark market for replayable, independently reproducible defensive improvements.

cards.title: Cyber-Sovereign Evidence Graph & Benchmark-to-Production RSI Flywheel
cards.thesis: Convert benchmark and production evidence into a durable graph that improves future routing, review, selection, and reinvestment.
cards.plain: Proof Card 011 is the evidence-graph card. It links missions, artifacts, ProofBundles, evaluator reports, reviewer attestations, red-team challenges, cost/risk ledgers, AGIALPHA settlement, delayed outcomes, and future routing decisions.
cards.why: A page can be copied. A long-running evidence graph cannot be retroactively fabricated. The execution moat is the living memory of what actually worked and under which constraints.
cards.mission: Build an evidence graph that connects benchmark evidence to production routing, delayed outcomes, reviewer reliability, settlement memory, and future RSI decisions.
